title: Fig. 1 Fig. 2
subject: Mathematics
objective:
To form a frustum of a cone.   Geometry   box,   sketch   pens,
cellotape, acrylic sheet, cutter.
procedure:
1. Take an acrylic sheet of a convenient size and cut out a circle of suitable
radius from it [see Fig.1].
2. Cut out a sector of, say 120º angle, from the circle [see Fig.2].
3. Make a cone from this sector by joining the two ends along the radii of the
sector as shown in Fig. 3.
observation:
On actual measurement:
1. Base of the triangle = ------- units.
2. Height of triangle = ------- units (i.e., radius of the circle).
3. Area of triangle =   1
2   (Base × Height) sq. units.
4. Area of circle = Area of triangle = -------------------.
discussion:
This result can be used in finding areas of
flower beds of circular and semi-circular
shapes and also for making circular designs and
in estimating the number of circular tiles
required to cover a floor.
